revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amamikyu]][[fr:Amamikyu]][[ja:阿摩美久]][[zh:阿摩美久]] {{Infobox deity}}
{{Short description|Creation goddess of the Ryukyu Islands in the Ryukyuan religion|qq=}}[[File:Tomb of Amamichu 03.JPG|thumb|right|Tomb of Amamikyu]] {{Nihongo|'''Amamikyu'''|阿摩美久<ref>Shō Shōken. ''Chūzan Seikan'' (in Japanese). 1650.</ref> or 阿摩彌姑<ref>Tei Heitetsu. ''Kyūyō'' (in Chinese). 1745.</ref>||extra={{langx|ryu|アマミチュー|qq=}} Amamichuu<ref name="okinawan">{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN00669|title=アマミチューシニリチュー|website=首里・那覇方言音声データベース|qq=}}</ref>|qq=}}, or {{Nihongo|'''Amekushin-otome-ōankami'''|天久臣乙女王御神|qq=}}, is the [[:en:Creation goddess|Creation goddess]] of the {{ill|Ryukyu Islands|en|Ryukyu Islands|qid=Q6897416|lt=Ryukyu Islands|1=Ryukyu Islands}} in the [[Ryukyuan religion]]. ==Name== Amamikyu's name comes from the reading of the {{ill|Chinese characters|en|Chinese characters|qid=Q8201|lt=Chinese characters|1=Chinese characters}} 阿摩美久 or 阿摩彌姑, which were most likely written {{ill|Ad hoc|en|Ad hoc|qid=Q192683|lt=Ad hoc|1=Ad hoc}} for the {{ill|Okinawan language|en|Okinawan language|qid=Q34233|lt=Okinawan pronunciation|1=Okinawan language}}. It is likely related to the name of the {{ill|Amami Islands|en|Amami Islands|qid=Q453510|lt=Amami Islands|1=Amami Islands}}. There are also kanji spellings of 天御子 and 天美子.<ref>{{cite-book|last=Hokama|first=Shūzen|year=1995|title=沖縄古語大辞典|trans-title=Dictionary of Archaic Okinawan|location=Tokyo|publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]]|page=38|qq=}}</ref> Readings can vary widely from Amamikyu, Amamikyo, Amamikiyo, Amamiko, Amamiku, Amamigu, Amamichuu, and Amanchuu. "Amamikyu" was used by {{ill|George H. Kerr|en|George H. Kerr|qid=Q715643|lt=George H. Kerr|1=George H. Kerr}} in his ''Ryukyu: Kingdom and Province Before 1945'' in 1953.<ref>Kerr, George. ''Okinawa:History of an Island People''. Tokyo: Charles E. Tuttle Publishing, 1958. 36.</ref> ==Creation myth== The beginning of ''{{ill|Chūzan Seikan|en|Chūzan Seikan|qid=Q8015936|lt=Chūzan Seikan|1=Chūzan Seikan}}'' details the creation of the Ryukyu Islands. The Heavenly Emperor (天帝), who lived in the Heavenly {{ill|Gusuku|en|Gusuku|qid=Q1044204|lt=Gusuku|1=Gusuku}} (天城), looked down on the world and saw that there were no islands, so he ordered Amamikyu (阿摩美久) to create the Ryukyu Islands. She asked for materials to build the islands, so the Heavenly Emperor sent Shinerikyu to bring her grasses, trees, and stones. She descended to Earth on {{ill|Kudaka Island|en|Kudaka Island|qid=Q4518835|lt=Kudaka Island|1=Kudaka Island}}, and then made landfall on {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Island|1=Okinawa Island}} on the spot of [[Sefa-utaki]], and later built {{ill|Tamagusuku Castle|en|Tamagusuku Castle|qid=Q11572469|lt=Tamagusuku Castle|1=Tamagusuku Castle}} and {{ill|Chinen Castle|en|Chinen Castle|qid=Q30926461|lt=Chinen Castle|1=Chinen Castle}} and a number of communities. She asked the Heavenly Emperor for materials to make people, but the other gods would not go down to Earth. Without sexual intercourse, she became pregnant by Shinerikyu (志仁禮久, {{langx|ryu|シニリチュー|qq=}} ''shinirichuu''<ref name="okinawan"/>) and populated the islands. Some generations later, a "heavenly grandchild" named Tentei was born, who split Ryukyuan society into five classes with his three sons and two daughters: the first son was [[List of monarchs of Ryukyu Islands#Tenson Dynasty|Tenson]], who became the first King of Ryukyu; the second son became the first feudal lord ([[:en:Anji (Ryukyu)|Aji]]); the third son became the first farmer; the first daughter became the first royal [[noro (priestess)|noro priestess]]; and the second daughter became the first village noro priestess.<ref>Glacken, Clarence. ''The Great Loochoo''. University of California Press, 1955. Pp 29-30.</ref> Her final home was located at [[Minton Castle]] in {{ill|Tamagusuku, Okinawa|en|Tamagusuku, Okinawa|qid=Q5119059|lt=Tamagusuku, Okinawa|1=Tamagusuku, Okinawa}}. ==Historical legacy== Amamikyu's tomb is located on {{ill|Hamahiga Island|en|Hamahiga Island|qid=Q4518764|lt=Hamahiga Island|1=Hamahiga Island}} in [[:en:Uruma, Okinawa|Uruma, Okinawa]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nifee.net/piman/ha-rekisi.html|title = 近所の琉球歴史|qq=}}</ref> Sefa-utaki is the holiest [[utaki]] site in the Ryukyuan religion. During the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|qid=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}} era, the king and {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|qid=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi|1=Kikoe-ōgimi}} made an annual {{ill|Pilgrimage|en|Pilgrimage|qid=Q1644573|lt=Pilgrimage|1=Pilgrimage}} to the site from {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|qid=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}} to worship Amamikyu, facing {{ill|Kudaka Island|en|Kudaka Island|qid=Q4518835|lt=Kudaka Island|1=Kudaka Island}}. ==See also== *[[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] *{{ill|Imra|en|Imra|qid=Q6008085|lt=Imra|1=Imra}} ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Jmyth navbox long|qq=}}
[[en:Amamikyu]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fr:Amamikyu]] [[ja:アーマンチュウメー]] [[zh:阿摩美久]] {{wikidata link|Q18236531}} [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:琉球の神]]
